text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy little town, lived a fox named Freddy. Freddy had big eyes and a cunning mind. He loved sunsets and would spend his afternoons strolling through the park, listening to the crickets sing.

One day, he overheard a squirrel talking about an enchanted oak tree that promised wishes. Impressed, Freddy collected his handful of wishes. His first was for endless summer days. He never tired. His second was for a cozy bed at the foot of a mountain unicorn. Sleeping would never hurt him.

His third wish was to be gallant. Maybe he wouldn'y win any awards, but he'd sure try.

Last was the grand wish: For a friend.

One afternoon, while munching on some berries, Freddy noticed a fox clutching something tight. Curious, he watched as the fox whispered something into a peculiar-looking twig. The fox disappeared, and Freddy snatched the twig.
